top_risk: Karma collapses into a second activity counter, gameable by mutual-boosting/alts, so it duplicates XP rather than adding a distinct signal.
cheapest_evidence: Ship a capped, log-only karma grant behind a flag and correlate karma vs XP rankings after a few weeks; low correlation + non-trivial grant volume validates distinctness.
report:
## Probe Report
### (1) Core claim
Adds a person-granted reputation score to a bot with XP (activity) and coins (economy). Claim: peer-granted reputation is a distinct, worthwhile signal, distinguished by the source of value (other members, not the bot).
### (2) Strongest failure mode
Karma degenerates into a second activity counter (distinctness collapse) AND is adversarially captured (collusion, mutual-boosting rings, sybil/alt farming). Open-probe mitigations (rate-limiting, caps, anti-collusion, spendable-or-not) are not measured in source.
### (3) Falsification / cheapest evidence
Rank-correlate karma vs XP over a trial window; high correlation falsifies distinctness. Inspect grant graph for reciprocity clusters, new-account bursts, concentration. Cheapest: log-only/shadow mode behind a flag, compute correlation offline.
### (4) Cost vs payoff
Modest schema/command/leaderboard cost; real cost is anti-abuse (can grow unbounded). Payoff contingent on distinctness (unproven).
### (5) Smallest slice
Peer grant with hard daily cap per grantor, no self-grants, per-pair cooldown, purely reputational (non-spendable), append-only grant events, NO leaderboard in v1 (log/shadow scores). Turn on leaderboard only after distinctness correlation clears a pre-agreed bar.
